title: nematolepis ovatifolia
intro: Nematolepis ovatifolia, also known as the golden bells or bell-fruit, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the southeastern region of Australia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Nematolepis ovatifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. Nematolepis ovatifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
